mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the S&P 500 exhibits negative 20-day forward returns because prolonged zero-rate policy signals structural demand deficiency and Fed impotence that outweighs discount-rate support.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 50% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate signals a broad contractionary gap that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate revenue and margin expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and rises in UNRATE are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions when unemployment is below 6%, a month-over-month decline in housing starts predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because housing weakness signals impending demand contraction and Fed overtightening risk.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6%, monthly declines in HOUST are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding 0.3% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because markets reprice near-term Fed normalization risk, raising the equity discount rate.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases above 0.3% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because stronger aggregate output supports corporate revenue expansion and reduces recession probability priced into equities.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and consumer inflation is below 2%, the joint combination of cheap money and stable prices predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because corporate borrowing costs remain suppressed without offsetting wage-price pressure.
failure_condition: In periods satisfying the trigger, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur less than 55% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because labor market deterioration signals declining household income and consumption, reducing corporate earnings visibility.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2pp over 3 months are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of triggered episodes over any 3-year rolling window.
